behavior_summary: Provides the item's response to the 'U8P_AXE::look' (inspect/examine) action: when the player examines the axe, the script displays a single short bark text "axe ".
Immediately after emitting the bark the process suspends/ends, so no further interaction or branching occurs.
No parameters, conditions, flags, or external calls are used; this is a minimal inspect/display handler.

Item metadata (external to the process) indicates overlay.animationStyle = 'axe' and usedBy = ['axe','deceiver'], which likely affect rendering/usage contexts but are not referenced by the script logic.
notable_control_flow: U8P_AXE::look emits one Bark node containing the text "axe " and then hits a suspend/terminate node, yielding a single-step display-then-stop flow.
There are no conditional branches, menu interactions, or calls to other functions; control flow is linear and terminal.
open_questions: Does the 'suspend' node here represent a temporary pause or a definitive end/return of the U8P_AXE::look process in engine terms for this object?
Is the trailing space in the bark text "axe " intentional (formatting for concatenation) or an artifact of extraction?
How (and where) the item-level metadata fields overlay.animationStyle and usedBy are consumed at runtime — by engine/item renderer or by other scripts — is not specified in this snippet.

main_function.name: U8P_AXE::look
main_function.purpose: Display the axe's short description/label to the player when they inspect the item, then end the U8P_AXE::look process.
entry_conditions: Triggered by the engine when the player uses the 'U8P_AXE::look' (inspect/examine) action on the axe item.
Implicit entry via the item's U8P_AXE::look/inspect handler (process type 0x0208).

function_map.name: U8P_AXE::look
function_map.role: inspect/display handler for the axe item (emits a single bark and suspends)

quick_facts.behavior_summary_one_liner: Provides the item's response to the 'U8P_AXE::look' (inspect/examine) action: when the player examines the axe, the script displays a single short bark text "axe ".
